title: 2-step Polymerase Chain Reaction (PCR)
author: Dana Mozaffari
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.7zrhp56

steps.title: Set up Thermocycler Program 1 (35 cycles)
steps.instruction: Set up the thermocycler program For initial denaturation 98°C for 2 minutes For 35 cycles: 98°C for 30 seconds for denaturation 30 seconds for primers annealing at temperature depending on primers and polymerase 72°C for 30 seconds per kb of DNA for elongation Final extension: 72°C for 7 minutes 4°C on hold

steps.title: Set up Thermocycler Program 2 (10 cycles)
steps.instruction: Set up the thermocycler program For initial denaturation 98°C for 30 seconds For 10 cycles: 98°C for 8 seconds for denaturation 20 seconds for primers annealing at temperature depending on primers and polymerase 72°C for 30 seconds per kb of DNA for elongation Final extension: 72°C for 7 minutes 4°C on hold

steps.title: Set up Thermocycler Program 3 (35 cycles)
steps.instruction: Set up the thermocycler program For initial denaturation 98°C for 2 minutes For 35 cycles: 98°C for 30 seconds for denaturation 30 seconds for primers annealing at temperature depending on primers and polymerase 72°C for 30 seconds per kb of DNA for elongation Final extension: 72°C for 7 minutes 4°C on hold

steps.title: Prepare Master Mix
steps.instruction: Prepare the master mix using the quantities specified in Materials

steps.title: Control PCR with Gel Electrophoresis
steps.instruction: Control the PCR by doing an agarose gel electrophoresis (follow Gel Electrophoresis Protocol)

steps.title: Load PCR and Start Program
steps.instruction: Load the PCR mixture in thermocycler and start program.

steps.title: Prepare Gene-Specific Master Mix
steps.instruction: Prepare the master mix with the gene specific PCR product and using the quantities specified in Materials

steps.title: Load Reaction and Start Program
steps.instruction: Load the reaction mixture in the thermocycler and start the program

steps.title: Control PCR with Gel Electrophoresis (2)
steps.instruction: Control the PCR by doing an agarose gel electrophoresis (follow Gel Electrophoresis Protocol)

steps.title: Purify PCR Product
steps.instruction: Purifiy the PCR product with a DNA purification kit

steps.title: Add Primers for Final PCR
steps.instruction: When the extension PCR is done, directly add in the tubes the primers for the final PCR with the quantities specified in Materials. Minimize the waiting time before starting the second reaction to keep polymerase activity high
